title: verbascum virgatum subsp. lusitanicum
intro: Verbascum virgatum subsp. lusitanicum, also known as the Iberian mullein or mullein, is a biennial herb native to the Iberian Peninsula in southwestern Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of saponins and mucilages. The health benefits of Verbascum virgatum subsp. lusitanicum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, and to treat skin conditions like eczema and wounds. The plant can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies like salves and ointments to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
